headlines.shortLinkText: Mitchell scores 24 as Cavaliers use fast start to beat Hornets 94-87

headlines.description: — Jaylen Brown had 30 points and 10 rebounds and the Boston Celtics opened a 23-point first-half lead before coasting to a 119-104 victory over the Indiana Pacers on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jaylen Brown scores 30 points, grabs 10 rebounds to lead Celtics to 119-104 victory over Pacers

events.name: Brooklyn Nets at New York Knicks

highlights.headline: Knicks crush Nets by 54 points for largest win in franchise history
highlights.description: The Knicks snap a four game losing streak and defeat the Nets by 54 points for their largest win in franchise history.

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Knicks crush Nets by 54 points for largest win in franchise history) 2026/01/22 ESHEET

headlines.description: — The New York Knicks broke a four-game losing streak without breaking a sweat, crushing the Brooklyn Nets 120-66 on Wednesday night in the most lopsided victory in franchise history.
headlines.shortLinkText: Knicks crush Nets 120-66 to end their 4-game skid with the most lopsided win in franchise history

video.headline: Knicks crush Nets by 54 points for largest win in franchise history

headlines.description: — Jalen Johnson had 32 points and 15 rebounds and a key basket in the closing minute as the Atlanta Hawks used a fourth-quarter burst and held on to defeat the Memphis Grizzlies 124-122 on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jalen Johnson has 32 points, 15 rebounds to lead the Hawks to a 124-122 victory over the Grizzlies

headlines.description: — Jalen Duran scored 20 points and grabbed 15 rebounds, Daniss Jenkins added 17 points and the Detroit Pistons never trailed while cruising to their fourth straight victory, 112-104 over the New Orleans Pelicans on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pistons never trail while cruising to fourth straight win, 112-104 over struggling Pelicans

headlines.description: — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 40 points, 11 assists and seven rebounds and the NBA-leading Oklahoma City Thunder withstood multiple injury-related absences to trounce the Milwaukee Bucks 122-102 on Wednesday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 40 points as short-handed Thunder roll past Bucks 122-102
